_meta.voice_register: Institutional B2B, Singapore-government-adjacent (BCA / PDPC / IHRP register). Observation over aspiration. No marketing-speak, no emojis, no exclamation marks in product copy.
_meta.positioning: BYO-deck reverse-engineering LMS — drop your existing training material, get modular units + AI-generated quizzes within hours. S$0 to S$299 + S$2.99/user/mo. No grant pitch.
_meta.section_keys_status: v2.0 — full rewrite for new positioning. 62 sections across 9 pages all bound to sitemap.json section ids.
rewrite_notes: Government training grants removed from all pitch copy per recalibrated brief.
Anti-audience framing (deskless, shift-based, no-email workforces) fully fenced off in pitch copy.

Grant-adjacent partner-status credentials removed from the credential strip — replaced with audit-trust credentials (Singapore-incorporated, PDPA-aligned hosting, audit-log export, IHRP HR-data baseline, operated in-house first).
Competitor-positioning copy on the for-whom anti-audience section retains references to deskless-focused vendors (EdApp, Disprz) in legitimate negative-positioning context only.

global.site_tagline: Drop your decks. We turn them into training.
global.site_description: Singapore-built LMS that reverse-engineers your existing decks into modular training and AI-generated quizzes. From S$0; S$2.99 per user when you go paid.

home.hero_headline: Your existing decks, turned into training in hours.
home.hero_subhead: Upload your sales decks, training decks, handbooks and product catalogues. We reverse-engineer them into modular units with AI-generated quizzes. Free up to 5 seats; S$2.99 per user a month when you go paid.
home.hero_proof_line: Operated by Elitez Group of Companies. Singapore-incorporated, PDPA-aligned, SG-region hosting. Used internally across ~200 staff since Q2 2026.

sections.note: Hero copy lives at page level (hero_eyebrow / hero_headline / hero_subhead / hero_proof_line / hero_cta_*). This entry is kept so renderers iterating sections do not skip it.

sections.headline: Built and operated to Singapore audit standards.
sections.body: Singapore-incorporated entity, SG-region hosting on PDPA-aligned infrastructure, IHRP HR-data baseline observed in access-control design, audit-log export available on Enterprise. Operated by Elitez Group of Companies — used in-house across ~200 staff before being offered externally.

sections.headline: Six things this LMS does that a generic SaaS LMS does not.
sections.body: We do not ask you to rebuild courses slide-by-slide. We do not ask you to write quizzes by hand. We do not ask you to host SG employee data offshore. We start with the materials you already have.
sections.items_source: value_props.items[] — render the six tiles from there.

sections.headline: Two doors: ready-made library, or your own decks.
sections.body: Door one is the Elitez library — 56 modules and 1,200+ questions covering SG HR compliance, recruitment craft, AI literacy, cyber hygiene and productivity. Door two is your own content — PowerPoint, PDF, DOCX, Google Slides or Notion exports. We ingest, structure and quiz against what you upload.

sections.headline: Day-1 to 90-day plans for the white-collar roles you already hire.
sections.body: Five tracks ship with the platform — Recruitment Consultant, BD Executive, Account Manager, HR Operations, and a Default track for everyone else. Non-admin staff see only what is scoped to their role. Admins see the full library. Your team does not need to author the plan.

sections.headline: Stand up an AI-use policy before your compliance officer asks for one.
sections.body: Five modules cover LLM concepts, prompting craft, AI risks, an AI-use-at-work policy template aligned to the IMDA Model AI Governance Framework, and day-to-day GenAI usage. IMDA's 2024 survey put SG white-collar GenAI adoption at 73 per cent against only 28 per cent of employers with a written policy. This slab closes that gap.

sections.headline: Four ways customers put the platform to work in week one.
sections.body: We see four repeatable shapes in the first 30 days: turn-key day-one library, BYO deck reverse-engineering, role-tracked onboarding, and white-label channel delivery for HRMS / EOR partners. Each one has a tier mapping in the pricing page.

items.label: Day-one library
items.detail: Use the 56-module Elitez library as the baseline; layer your own decks on top later. Starts at Team — S$2.99/user/mo.

items.label: BYO-deck reverse-engineering
items.detail: Drop 5 to 8 of your decks; we return modular units within hours. Pro tier — S$99 platform + S$2.99/user/mo.

items.label: Role-tracked onboarding
items.detail: Build paths per job family with knowledge-gap heatmaps and per-question analytics. Scale tier — S$199 + S$2.99/user/mo.

items.label: White-label channel delivery
items.detail: Your brand, your domain, your SSO, our engine. Enterprise tier — S$299 + S$2.99/user/mo.

sections.body: Numbers reflect the live production library on this date.

sections.headline: Send a deck. We will turn it into a working module.
sections.body: Book a 15-minute walkthrough. Bring one of your existing decks. You will leave the call with a published module and a draft quiz against your own content.

product.hero_headline: A library you can use today and a pipeline that ingests your own decks.
product.hero_subhead: 56 modules and 1,200+ questions out of the box. Drop your PPT, PDF, DOCX, Google Slides or Notion exports — we reverse-engineer them into modular units with AI-generated quizzes against the uploaded content.

sections.note: Hero copy lives at page level.

sections.headline: 56 modules and 1,200+ questions, all SG-relevant.
sections.body: The Elitez baseline library covers SG HR compliance, recruitment craft, AI literacy, cyber hygiene and productivity. Every module ships with quizzes. Every question is authored for a Singapore workplace context.

sections.headline: Five content slabs, browsable below.
sections.body: SG HR compliance covers Employment Act Part IV, CPF, work passes EP/SP/WP, TAFEP fair consideration, KETs, salary, leave, termination, retrenchment, IR8A, IR21, WSH, TADM and Re-employment Act. Recruitment craft covers Boolean search, LinkedIn sourcing, candidate interview, BANT, MEDDIC, outreach, proposals and account management. AI literacy covers LLM concepts, prompting, AI risks and AI-at-work policy. Cyber hygiene covers phishing, password, MFA, BYOD and incident reporting. Productivity covers Google Workspace, Excel, PowerPoint, PDF tooling, Sign Now and meeting hygiene.

sections.headline: Five tracks ship with the platform.
sections.body: Recruitment Consultant, BD Executive, Account Manager, HR Operations, and a Default track. On Scale and above, you build additional tracks per job family. Tracks are audience-scoped — non-admin staff see only what is keyed to their role.

sections.headline: RAG progress, per-module breakdown, per-question heatmaps.
sections.body: Managers see M1 / M2 / M3 progress in red-amber-green against their direct reports. On Pro and above, per-module and per-question analytics surface which content needs work. On Scale, knowledge-gap heatmaps show which questions teams consistently get wrong. Exports run to CSV from Pro; API from Scale.

sections.headline: Ship an AI policy your D&O carrier will recognise.
sections.body: Five modules — LLM basics, prompt craft, AI risks, AI-at-work policy template, day-to-day GenAI usage. The policy template is structured against the IMDA Model AI Governance Framework v2 (2024) and the PDPC advisory on use of personal data in AI systems. Adopt as written or fork into your own tone.

sections.headline: Completion evidence your auditor accepts.
sections.body: Completion records are timestamped, attributed and exportable. On Enterprise, the audit log is tamper-evident and surfaces every administrator action. Built to satisfy Companies Act record-keeping expectations for duty-of-care evidence in regulated verticals.

sections.headline: Browser-based, English-first, SG-hosted.
sections.body: Application and learner data hosted on SG-region infrastructure. Email-OTP sign-in at base; SSO with SAML, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 on Enterprise. HRIS integrations (Talenox, JustLogin, BambooHR) plus Slack, Teams and Notion on Enterprise. CSV export from Pro; REST API from Scale.

sections.headline: The fastest answer is a 15-minute walkthrough with one of your decks.
sections.body: We map your existing material against the Elitez library, mark the gap, and quote a tier on the spot.

pricing.hero_headline: Five tiers from S$0 to S$299 platform plus S$2.99 per user.
pricing.hero_subhead: Free up to 5 seats. Per-user pricing is constant at S$2.99 across all paid tiers — the platform-flat fee is what scales for capability, not the per-head charge. Month-to-month by default; annual discounts available privately on Scale and Enterprise.

sections.note: Hero copy lives at page level.

sections.headline: Pick by capability, not by seat count.
sections.body: Starter is free. Team is per-seat only. Pro, Scale and Enterprise add a platform-flat fee that unlocks uploads, AI quiz generation against your content, role tracks, custom theme, white-label and SSO. Seat fee is S$2.99 throughout.
sections.tiers_source: tiers[] — render the five tier cards from there.

sections.headline: What each tier includes side-by-side.
sections.body: Library access, BYO uploads, AI quiz generation against your content, manager dashboards, per-question analytics, role tracks, custom theme, white-label, SSO, API, HRIS integrations, audit log. Rows render against the tier matrix below.

sections.headline: Four rules behind the ladder.
sections.body: How we set the price and why the per-seat is constant.

items.label: Swipe-card friendly
items.detail: Up to roughly 50–100 seats, you should not need procurement to involve finance.

items.label: Constant per-seat
items.detail: S$2.99 per user, per month, across every paid tier. The platform-flat fee is what scales for capability.

items.label: Month-to-month default
items.detail: No annual lock-in on Starter, Team or Pro. Annual discounts are negotiated privately on Scale and Enterprise.

items.label: Your content stays yours
items.detail: You retain ownership of uploaded decks and AI-generated derivatives. We licence them solely to deliver training to your team.

sections.headline: Building a Learn module inside your HRMS or EOR stack?
sections.body: Enterprise tier includes white-label, custom domain, SSO, HRIS integrations and a dedicated Customer Success Manager. Channel partners get co-marketing collateral and a partner-margin discount on the platform fee. Per-seat stays at S$2.99 so end-customer economics remain predictable.

sections.headline: Questions on the ladder.
sections.body: Minimum seats, trial limits, upgrades, downgrades, content ownership, billing currency.
sections.items_source: faq[] — render the entries flagged with category 'pricing'.

sections.headline: Pick a tier or send a deck.
sections.body: Starter starts free. For anything else, a 15-minute walkthrough is the fastest way to land on the right tier.

for-whom.hero_subhead: SG SMEs and mid-market teams (30–500 headcount) with existing decks; EA-licensed recruitment agencies who need turn-key compliance content; HRMS, EOR and consultancy partners who want to white-label a learning module into their stack.

sections.note: Hero copy lives at page level.

sections.headline: EA-licensed recruitment agencies (15–150 staff).
sections.body: MOM EA Act, TAFEP, Fair Consideration, CPF and work-pass content ship out of the box. Role tracks for Recruitment Consultant, BD Executive, Account Manager and HR Operations are baseline. New recruiters land in a structured plan from day one; auditors get completion evidence in one click. Typical fit: Team or Pro tier.
buyer_signals: Compliance audit asks for completion evidence
Recruiter ramp is 8–12 weeks of fragmented training
Role onboarding lives in eight disconnected decks per role

sections.headline: HRMS, EOR and consultancy partners.
sections.body: Customers ask for learning. An in-house build is a 12-month roadmap. Off-the-shelf catalogues do not carry your brand. The Enterprise tier gives you a white-label LMS with custom domain, SSO, HRIS integrations and an API — your customers see your brand, never ours. Co-branded marketing collateral and partner-margin economics included.
buyer_signals: Customers asking for a Learn module on the roadmap
Off-the-shelf catalogue buys destroy bundle economics
PDPA + SG-region hosting is a procurement-clearing requirement

sections.headline: SG SMEs and mid-market teams with decks already written.
sections.body: Consultancies, advisories, law firms, design studios, professional-services partnerships, brand-led retail and F&B operators. Your onboarding deck, SOP pack and brand book already exist — they live in SharePoint or Google Drive and nobody reads them. Drop them into Pro; we return structured modules and AI-generated quizzes against your content within hours.
buyer_signals: Manager spends 5–15 hours a quarter answering the same training questions
Procurement bandwidth is zero — needs to be swipe-card-able

sections.headline: Match your shape to the tier ladder.
sections.body: Three buyer shapes, three typical landing tiers, three distinct entry pitches. Rows render: segment, primary pain, recommended tier, typical seat band, monthly all-in at the midpoint of that band.

sections.headline: Who this platform is not built for.
sections.body: Plain about the boundary so neither side wastes a call.

items.label: Deskless or frontline-only workforces
items.detail: We are browser-based and English-first. EdApp and Disprz cover that shape better — go to them for shift-worker delivery, not us.

items.label: Enterprises above ~5,000 seats
items.detail: If your Cornerstone, Workday or SAP SuccessFactors procurement is already in flight, finish that path. We are sized for SMEs and mid-market.

items.label: SMS-OTP-only or no-email workforces
items.detail: Authentication is email-OTP today; SSO on Enterprise. Workforces without email addresses are not a fit.

items.label: Buyers who only want a catalogue
items.detail: If you want to buy Coursera or Udemy seats and forward links, we are the wrong shape. We are an authoring + delivery platform, not a content marketplace.

sections.headline: Tell us which segment fits and we will tailor the demo.
sections.body: We will show the agency walkthrough, the channel-partner walkthrough or the BYO-deck walkthrough — your call.

about.hero_headline: Built by an operator, used in-house first.
about.hero_subhead: Elitez Group of Companies has operated in Singapore HR services since 2008. The LMS started as our internal training tool across ~200 staff. It is offered externally now because the BYO-deck reverse-engineering pipeline solved a problem we kept watching SG SMEs run into.

sections.note: Hero copy lives at page level.

sections.headline: How this platform got built.
sections.body: Three steps from a SharePoint folder to a productised LMS.

steps.detail: Elitez Group operates in Singapore HR services — staffing, consulting and outsourcing. Our own training content piles up in SharePoint and Google Drive.
We build an internal LMS for the ~200-strong Elitez team. The reverse-engineering pipeline shortens content authoring from weeks to hours.

steps.label: 2025 onwards
steps.detail: SG SMEs we work with ask whether they can use it for their own decks. We harden the platform, write a multi-tenant tier ladder and open the door.

sections.headline: Singapore-incorporated and audit-ready.
sections.body: Operator credentials relevant to a buyer's procurement diligence.

items.label: Operated in-house first

sections.headline: What we operate alongside the LMS.
sections.body: Elitez Group runs adjacent businesses in staffing, consulting and outsourcing. Our customer base shaped both the Elitez baseline library and the BYO-deck pipeline.

items.label: Staffing operations
items.detail: EA-licensed recruitment desks across Singapore. The Elitez library reflects what those desks need to ramp recruiters in 4 weeks.

items.label: Consulting
items.detail: Onboarding, SOP and L&D consulting for SG SMEs and mid-market firms. The platform is where the work ends up.

items.label: Outsourcing
items.detail: HR-Ops and admin outsourcing for clients. Our HR-Ops role track is the same one we use internally.

sections.headline: Who builds and runs the platform.
sections.body: Engineering, content and product run out of Elitez Group's Singapore HQ at 2 Kallang Avenue. The platform is led by Derrick Teo, Director of Elitez Group of Companies. Direct contact: derrick@elitez.asia.

sections.headline: Three quick destinations.
sections.body: Pick one of these to keep going.

admin.hero_subhead: Top-5 threats, the full 30-entity table, 8-D strategy canvas radar and per-competitor detail panels. Sourced from /data/intel/competitors.json and /data/intel/whitespace-framework.json.

sections.note: Internal page. Hero copy is functional, not promotional.

sections.headline: Five competitors to track this quarter.
sections.body: Coursebox AI, Articulate 360, iSpring Learn, Talenox Learn, and DIY SharePoint + Forms. Cards hydrate from competitors.json.top_five with each competitor's rationale, strengths, weaknesses and threat level.

sections.headline: 30 competitors, filterable by category and threat level.
sections.body: Renders the full /data/intel/competitors.json entries with columns: name, category, HQ, target market, SG monthly equivalent (SGD), pricing flag, threat level, beatability.

sections.headline: Strategy canvas — eight dimensions.
sections.body: BYO-deck ingest, AI quiz from your content, SG HR-baseline content, SG/PDPA residency, sub-S$300 platform band, white-label / partner-ready, per-question analytics, self-serve swipe-card buy. Radar overlay of us vs Coursebox AI, Articulate, iSpring, Talenox Learn, DIY SharePoint, Docebo, Disprz, TalentLMS, Cornerstone.

sections.headline: Per-competitor drill-down.
sections.body: Clicking any competitor row opens a modal with full record: value proposition, features, strengths, weaknesses, pricing band, market share estimate, website design rating, findability seconds for pricing/demo/contact.

sections.headline: Global incumbents, regional challengers, SG-local, DIY alternatives, adjacent.
sections.body: Counts and lists per category — global_incumbent (Articulate, Coursebox, iSpring, Docebo, TalentLMS, 360Learning, Tovuti, Sana, Synthesia, HeyGen, Cornerstone, SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, BambooHR, Deel Engage, Multiplier), regional_challenger (Disprz), sg_local (CloudKampus, Skillgrasp, Wizlearn, ConnectAll, Talenox Learn, JustLogin Learning), diy_alternative (SharePoint + Forms, Google Drive + Forms, Notion + Tally, WhatsApp), adjacent (KnowBe4, Coursera for Business).

admin-insights.hero_subhead: TAM/SAM/SOM funnel, six SG policies, country readiness, five personas with NBA cards, five-tier ladder, segment-need heatmap, five attack plans, six market trends. Sourced from /data/intel/market-intelligence.json, /data/intel/pricing-strategy.json and /data/intel/whitespace-framework.json.

sections.note: Internal page. Hero copy is functional.

sections.headline: S$62M TAM, S$27M SAM, S$1.9M SOM (Y3 exit).
sections.body: Three-stage funnel from market-intelligence.json. TAM stack: mid-market 30–500 HC (S$30M) + sub-enterprise 500–2,000 HC (S$18M) + provider tenancy (S$14M). SAM filters: ≥30 HC, browser-based workforce, deck-heavy material, SaaS-procurement-ready, PDPA-sensitive. SOM models direct + EA-agency seed Y1, HRMS/EOR channel Y2, multi-entity SMB consolidators + Enterprise white-label Y3.

sections.headline: Six SG regulatory anchors.
sections.body: PDPA SG-residency expectations, IMDA AI Verify Foundation, MOM TAFEP Fair Consideration Framework, IHRP HR-data PDPA guidance, Companies Act + ACRA audit-trail expectations, IRAS IR8A/IR21 documentation hygiene. Each card surfaces implication-for-us from market-intelligence.json.policies[].

sections.headline: SG primary, regional in scope.
sections.body: Per-country regulatory / tech-maturity / price-tolerance scores from market-intelligence.json.adoption_patterns.country_readiness. Singapore (5/5/4), Hong Kong (4/5/4), Malaysia (3/4/3), Indonesia (3/3/2), Thailand (3/3/2), Vietnam (2/3/2).

sections.headline: Five buyer shapes.
sections.body: SME Ops Director with decks, EA-agency Operations Head, HRMS / EOR partner CS Lead, Mid-market HR Manager (100–500 HC), Brand-led SME owner. Each card includes ICP, pains, current workaround, willingness-to-pay band and recommended tier.

sections.headline: Monthly SGD equivalent each persona is paying today.
sections.body: NBA arithmetic from pricing-strategy.json. SME Ops Director ~S$600/mo (manager time at S$60/hr × 10 hrs/mo); EA-agency Ops Head ~S$600/mo (12 hrs/mo at S$50/hr); HRMS partner CS Lead ~S$1,520/mo (Articulate + Coursera + integration eng); Mid-market HR Manager ~S$830/mo (TalentLMS + Articulate + reporting time); Brand-led SME owner ~S$700/mo (design agency + Synthesia + WordPress).

sections.headline: Starter, Team, Pro, Scale, Enterprise.
sections.body: Full tier table from pricing-strategy.json.recommended_tiers — price, minimum seats, what is included, what is excluded, psychological anchor. Verbatim from intel; do not edit on this page.

sections.headline: Empty by design.
sections.body: pricing-strategy.json.grants is intentionally an empty array. Government grants are excluded from the marketing pitch on the recalibrated brief. Mentioned privately in sales conversations if a buyer raises them; not surfaced on any external page.

sections.headline: Five segments × six needs.
sections.body: Segments: SME Ops Director with decks, EA-agency Operations Head, HRMS/EOR partner CS Lead, Mid-market HR Manager, Brand-led SME owner. Needs: ingest existing decks, auto-quiz from your content, SG-compliance baseline, white-label, per-question heatmap, swipe-card buy. Cells hydrate from whitespace-framework.json.heatmap.cells with us-score plus top-4 competitor specialisations per cell.

sections.headline: Five wedges, ranked.
sections.body: Rank 1: BYO-deck reverse-engineering (S$10M TAM). Rank 2: EA-agency turn-key onboarding kit (S$3M TAM). Rank 3: HRMS / EOR white-label Learn module (S$6M TAM). Rank 4: Mid-market role-tracks + heatmaps (S$11M TAM). Rank 5: Brand-precious SME white-label wedge (S$4M TAM). Each plan surfaces ICP, why-gap, why-we-win, GTM channel, pitch, pricing, content.

sections.headline: Six market signals shaping the wedge.
sections.body: Source-driven vs prompt-driven AI authoring bifurcation; HRMS-bundled learning squeeze; per-question analytics displacing DIY; AI-policy training as 2026 mandatory; white-label + multi-tenant becoming channel-partner expectation; voice/avatar AI video fragmenting authoring. Implications from market-intelligence.json.trends[].

items.title: Reverse-engineer your existing decks
items.body: Drop PPT, PDF, DOCX, Google Slides or Notion exports. We ingest, structure and chunk them into modular training units within hours — not the three weeks a slide-by-slide rebuild would cost.

items.title: AI-generated quizzes from your uploaded content
items.body: Quiz items are written against the deck you uploaded, not from generic prompts. Multiple-choice format. Manager review and edit on first publish; you keep the final say.

items.title: 1,200+ ready-made SG-relevant questions
items.body: The Elitez baseline library ships 56 modules and 1,200+ questions covering SG HR compliance, recruitment craft, AI literacy, cyber hygiene and productivity. Included from the entry tier.

items.title: Branded delivery, white-label on Enterprise
items.body: Pro and Scale carry your logo, theme and brand colours. Enterprise adds a custom domain and removes Elitez branding entirely — learners never see we are underneath.

items.title: Manager dashboards with role-keyed RAG progress
items.body: M1 / M2 / M3 progress in red-amber-green against role tracks. Per-question analytics from Pro. Knowledge-gap heatmaps from Scale. CSV export from Pro; API from Scale.

items.title: PDPA-aligned, SG-region residency
items.body: Application and learner data sit on SG-region infrastructure. Audit-log support on Enterprise. Procurement-clearing residency story for buyers whose vendors today are hosted in the US, AU or India.

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.target_persona_label: Solo founders and tiny teams kicking the tires
tiers.summary: Read-only access to the Elitez library — see whether the SG-compliance baseline fits before you spend anything.
what_in: Read-only access to the Elitez library (56 modules, 1,200+ questions)

tiers.name: Team
tiers.target_persona_label: EA agencies and SMEs using the Elitez library as their baseline
tiers.summary: Full library access with your logo and company name on the existing decks. No deck uploads at this tier.
what_in: Your logo and company name on the existing decks
what_out: No AI quiz generation against your own content

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.target_persona_label: SMEs with decks who want them reverse-engineered into modules
tiers.summary: The BYO-deck tier. Upload 5 to 8 of your own decks; we return modular units with AI-generated quizzes against the uploaded content.
what_in: Upload 5 to 8 of your own decks; reverse-engineered into modules within hours
AI-generated quizzes against your uploaded content

tiers.name: Scale
tiers.target_persona_label: Mid-market HR teams (100–500 HC) and brand-led SMEs
tiers.summary: Up to 20 custom modules, role-based learning tracks, knowledge-gap heatmaps, custom theme, REST API for export.
what_in: Up to 20 custom modules from uploads
Role-based learning tracks (paths per job family)

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.target_persona_label: HRMS, EOR and consultancy partners with white-label use cases
tiers.summary: White-label with custom domain, SSO, HRIS integrations, unlimited custom modules, audit-log export, dedicated CSM.
what_in: White-label and custom domain (learners never see Elitez)
HRIS, Slack, Teams and Notion integrations

faq.q: What file formats can you ingest?
faq.a: PowerPoint (.pptx), PDF, Word (.docx), Markdown (.md), Google Slides export and Notion page export. If you have something else — Keynote, Pages, raw HTML — send it on the walkthrough call and we will confirm whether the pipeline handles it.

faq.q: How long does deck reverse-engineering take?
faq.a: Most decks land as published modules within a few hours. On paid tiers we publish an SLA — Pro and Scale return draft modules inside one business day, Enterprise inside four hours.

faq.q: What does the AI-generated quiz look like?
faq.a: Multiple-choice with single-correct or multi-select. Each question is grounded in a specific section of the deck you uploaded, with a source-reference for the manager to verify. Manager review and edit before first publish is the default — the AI never publishes to learners without a human pass.

faq.q: What if my deck is bad — outdated, sparse, inconsistent?
faq.a: The reverse-engineering pipeline surfaces gaps rather than inventing content. If a section is too thin to support a module, we flag it for the manager to fill in. We do not generate filler from prompts to cover for source weaknesses — that is a category we deliberately do not compete in.
No annual lock-in on Starter, Team or Pro. Month-to-month is the default. Annual commitments are negotiated privately on Scale and Enterprise where the discount mechanic is more typical.

Custom theme — your logo and brand colours — is available from Scale. Full white-label with a custom domain (your-brand.com/learn rather than lms.elitez.com.sg) is on Enterprise. On Enterprise, learners do not see Elitez branding anywhere.
Yes, on Enterprise. We support SAML 2.0, Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 (Entra ID). Pre-Enterprise tiers use email-OTP sign-in.

Application and learner data are hosted on Singapore-region infrastructure. PDPA-aligned. Our access-control design observes the IHRP HR-data PDPA refresh (2024). Tamper-evident audit logs are available on Enterprise.

CSV export of completion records, quiz responses and module analytics is available from Pro. A REST API for the same is available from Scale. Enterprise adds full audit-log export. You retain ownership of all uploaded content and exports.

faq.q: What does the trial look like?
faq.a: Starter is free up to 5 seats with no time limit. You get read-only access to the Elitez library so you can evaluate the SG-compliance baseline before deciding on a paid tier.

faq.q: Can you integrate with my HRMS — Talenox, JustLogin, BambooHR?
faq.a: HRMS integrations are on Enterprise. We currently support Talenox, JustLogin and BambooHR for user provisioning and completion sync. Slack, Teams and Notion integrations are included on Enterprise for nudges and content embeds.

faq.q: Who owns the content I upload?
faq.a: You do. You retain ownership of uploaded decks and any AI-generated derivatives. We licence the material solely to deliver training to your team on our platform. If you cancel, you can export everything and we remove the data on request.

Bring one of your existing decks (PPT or PDF). We ingest it live on the call, walk you through the resulting modular structure, show the AI-generated quiz, and quote a tier against your seat band. You leave with a published module you can share with your team.

Derrick Teo, Director of Elitez Group of Companies, replies from derrick@elitez.asia within one working day. The walkthroughs are run by the team that built and operates the platform — not a separate sales motion.
